title: Why my best friends are my best critics
primary_topics: agreeing to disagree with close friends
sub_topics: Antonio T. Carpio personal friendship despite disagreements
Salonga as guru / dean / surrogate father
Salonga's 'A Test of Courage' book honoring the Panganiban Court
Cosmopolitan Church (Salonga's faith) vs Catholic faith
PCP II (Second Plenary Council of the Philippines, February 1991)
priests and nuns marriage proposal at PCP II
wife Leni as closest friend and critic

stances.claim: He has differed without being difficult with all his best friends — divergences made in good faith and in gentle, though at times emphatic, language are signs of authentic friendship, not its absence.
stances.rhetorical_move: personal-ethic statement on disagreement + universal claim ('all, repeat all')

stances.claim: Offensive criticisms conceived in malice and bad faith — at imagined, false, or misleading personal defects — should be ignored; he will 'let them sink in their own mud' and stand on his track record, refusing to let schemers and kibitzers disrupt his goals.

Salonga's foremost teaching was not about law but about life — that the rare gems money cannot buy (excellence, integrity, honor, dignity, reverential regard and love for God) are far better than the necessities money can buy.
stances.rhetorical_move: transmission of mentor's life-lesson + value-hierarchy framing

stances.claim: Salonga was the best president our country never had — first in the bar, LLB-UP / LLM-Harvard / JSD-Yale, topped three senatorial elections — by comparison CJ 'pales utterly,' being only a poor newsboy from squalid Tondo with an FEU LLB and 6th place in the bar.
stances.rhetorical_move: comparative humility-trope + structured-credential elevation of the mentor

stances.claim: Friend first before critic, and love first before criticism — his wife Leni's critiques of his writing, speech, and appearance are valued precisely because 'every critical word is uttered with unconditional love.'

institutions: Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines
events: Second Plenary Council of the Philippines final sessions (mid-February 1991)

signature_phrases: I have differed without being difficult
I will simply let them sink in their own mud
the best president our country never had
rare gems that money CANNOT buy
excellence, integrity, honor, dignity, and a reverential regard and love for God
the Source of all that is true, good and beautiful
Friend first before critic, and love first before criticism
every critical word is uttered with unconditional love
register_markers: Well, I have news for them
St. Paul on the road to Damascus reference (quoted from Salonga)
decision_framework_signals: applies 95-percent harmony / 5-percent cacophony framework for evaluating friendships
uses Romans 12:17-19 as scriptural basis for not repaying evil with evil
frames criticism reception as bifurcated: good-faith friend critique (welcomed) vs malicious schemer attack (ignored)
uses 'friend first before critic' sequencing as relationship principle
cross_references: On my piece titled, 'Agreeing to disagree with Justice Carpio' (Oct. 27)
notable_anecdotes: Salonga at the launch of 'A Test of Courage' on January 22, 2008 calling CJ 'my mentor' — reversing their mentor-disciple direction — and comparing him to St. Paul on the road to Damascus
Salonga as 'a revered pastor in the Cosmopolitan Church' inviting but never proselytizing CJ, who 'was and still is just a fledgling Catholic'

Archbishop Legaspi repeatedly ruling CJ out of order at PCP II for insistently proposing and arguing that priests and nuns should be allowed to marry — yet later recommending him to Pope John Paul II for the Pontifical Council of the Laity
Wife Leni occasionally critiquing his writing, speech, and dress while remaining his closest friend and beloved
